titleStmt.title: SII 37.271: original edition by S. Nagarjuna and K. Munirathnam
No. 271. (A.R. No. B 377 of 1940-41.) Gollala uppalapāḍu, Jammalamaḍugu Taluk, Cuddapah District.

summary.p: Stone lying in front of Āñjanēya temple.
This is dated Śaka 1490, Rākshasa (wrong for Vibhava), Āshāḍa, śu. 11, corresponding to 1568 A.D., July 5, Monday.

It records a gift of land by Nārappa Nāyaningāru, the daḷa (probably Daḷavāyi) of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Nandēla Timmayyadēva Mahārāju to the Pipers and Drummers for services in the temple of Chennakēśava Perumāḷ at Gollala Uppalapāḍu situated in Ghaṇḍikōṭa-sīma which was given as nāyaṅkara to Timmayyadēva by the king.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu . svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śatava
rṣaṁbulu 1490 Agunēṁṭi rākṣasa saṁvatsara Āṣāḍha śu 11 lu . śrī rmama
hārājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa sadāśiva ma
hārāyalu vidyānagaramaṁdu ratna siṁhāsanārūḍhu
lai pṛdhivi sāṁbrājyaṁ sēyucuṁḍagānu Akhilāṁḍakōṭi
brahmāṁḍḍa nāyakuṁḍaina dēvadēvōttamuṁḍaina gollala vuppa
lapāṭi ceṁnnakēśavaperumāḷa divya śrī caraṇāraviṁdaṁbulaku
śrīmaṁnmahāmaṁḍalēśvara naṁdyēla tiṁmayadēvamahārājula
vāri daḷanārappanāyunigāruṁ daṁḍamulu samarpiṁcci yicina dharma
śāsanaṁ . svāmi sadāsivadēvamahārāyuluṁgāru naṁdēla tiṁmmayya
dēvamahārājulagārki nāyaṁkarānaku pāliṁcina gaṁḍḍikōṭa śīmalōni
gollala vuppalapāṭi grāmānanu tathātidhi pradhama yākadaśi puṇyakālamaṁdu
ceṁnnakēśaperumāḷaku nityānu vūḷigaṁ naṁdyēla nāraparājayyagārki puṇyaṁ
gānu ḍōlu nāgasarāla vūḷigaṁ sēsē Aṁduku mānyaṁ pālu cēnu regaṭa
ṁcēnu kha . 1 nnu cavuṭa cēnu paṭṇaṁ tervuna É4 sagapāluṁgānu mānyaṁ
ṁ caṁdrārkkastāyigāsavi mānyaṁ Aṁbhaviṁci perumāḷaku ḍōḷu vāru ja 2
nāgasarālavāru ja 2 nu kūḍanu nalugurunu rēpunu māpunu sālumunu pū
ḷagaṁ kodavagākuṁḍānu sēsukoni bradukagalavāru Ani vrāyiṁcci yi
ccina silāśāsanaṁ . Ñ maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrīṁ jēyunu
Digital edition of SII 37.271 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
